notes: Procedural generation tables for dungeon dressing, random events, quest hooks, and DoomClock threshold events. Entries are flavor text; mechanical effects are handled by engine.py.
dungeon_dressing: Charred beams lean against crumbling walls, their surfaces etched with the growth-rings of centuries.
A carpet of grey ash covers the floor, disturbed by recent footprints that are not yours.
Bioluminescent fungi glow sickly green along the ceiling, casting shadows that move when you don't.
Rusted chains hang from iron hooks set in the stone, the links fused together by corrosion.
A faint smell of sulfur and decay permeates the air, strongest near the floor.
Claw marks score the walls in parallel lines -- something was dragged through here. Recently.
An overturned cart spills corroded tools across the passage. The wood has begun to sprout mushrooms.
Water drips from a crack in the ceiling into a shallow pool that reflects no light.
The petrified bark walls bear the faded ghost of an Arborist mural -- a tree in bloom, half-obscured by fungal growth.
A collapsed doorframe reveals a room beyond, sealed by a wall of solidified amber. Something is preserved inside.
Roots push through the ceiling in thick tangles, their surfaces slick with dark sap.
The floor is covered in a thin layer of fine glass-like shards -- the remnants of shattered amber.
An old campfire ring holds cold ashes and the bones of a small animal, picked clean.
The walls here are warm to the touch. An Aether vein runs behind the stone, pulsing faintly.
A Seeker's pack lies abandoned against the wall. The straps are cut, not unbuckled.
Shelf-fungi grow from the walls in shelf-like formations, their edges dripping a clear, viscous liquid.
The air here is noticeably colder. Frost patterns trace the stone where the Ashenmere Root-Road passes deep below.
An ironbark door stands intact in a ruined frame, its surface carved with a symbol no one in Emberhome recognizes.
The corridor narrows to a crawl-space. Scratches on the stone show where others have squeezed through -- and where some got stuck.
A broken Arborist mechanism lies scattered across the floor -- gears, amber lenses, and copper wire, all corroded beyond function.
The ceiling here is impossibly high, vanishing into darkness. Occasional sounds drift down from above -- clicks, shuffles, the scrape of chitin.
A row of empty alcoves lines the wall, each sized to hold a humanoid figure. Three of the six are stained with grey residue.
The stone floor is cracked in a radial pattern, as if something heavy landed here from a great height.
A thin stream of golden sap trickles down the wall, pooling in a natural basin. The liquid glows faintly.
Someone has scratched a message into the wall at knee height: 'TURN BACK. THE SONG STOPS HERE.'
An intact Arborist light-sconce flickers on the wall, its amber lens casting a warm circle on the opposite stone. Everything outside the circle is darker than it should be.
The passage branches around a pillar of petrified heartwood, its grain still visible under centuries of dust. Both paths look equally dangerous.
A colony of pale, blind beetles covers the lower walls, flowing around obstacles like living water. They avoid the light.
The floor here is sticky with dried rot-fluid. Bootprints preserved in the residue lead deeper, then stop.
An amber-inlaid bench stands against the wall, perfectly preserved amid the ruin. It looks like it was placed here yesterday.
random_events: The DoomClock ticks forward -- distant rumbling shakes loose a rain of stone dust.
A rot-touched animal wanders past, ignoring the party. Its movements are mechanical, purposeless.
Ash rain begins falling from cracks in the ceiling, coating everything in a fine grey layer.
A distant scream echoes through the corridors. Human. Recent. Then silence.
The torches flicker and dim momentarily, as if something drew the warmth from the air.
The Root-Song shifts -- a pulse of urgency vibrates through the floor, then fades.
A Hollow shambles past a side corridor, carrying salvage in its arms. It does not notice the party.
The walls creak and groan as the Burnwillow thrashes in its sleep. A new crack splits the ceiling.
A faint amber glow pulses from behind a sealed wall. An Amber Vault is nearby.
The smell of woodsmoke reaches you from an impossible direction. Someone else is in the dungeon.
A Scrap Imp darts across the corridor clutching something shiny, chittering with glee.
The temperature drops sharply. Breath becomes visible. The cold lasts exactly ten heartbeats, then passes.
A section of floor gives way, revealing a lower passage. The drop is survivable. Probably.
An echo of the Root-Song carries a fragment of memory -- a glimpse of the tree in its Golden Age, green and whole.
A patrol of Charred Skeletons marches past in formation, following a route they walked in life. They do not deviate.
Spore-fall begins -- a slow drift of pale particles from fungal colonies above. The air tastes of copper.
The light from your torch catches something metallic embedded in the wall. A key? A trap mechanism? A relic?
A tremor shakes the corridor. When it stops, a previously sealed door stands slightly ajar.
The sound of running water comes from ahead -- an underground stream, or a ruptured Aether vein.
A Cinder Wolf howls in the distance. A second answers from a different direction. Then a third.
You find a Seeker's journal, its last entry dated three days ago: 'Found the Vault. Can't open it. Something follows.'
The fungi on the walls pulse in unison, brightening and dimming like a slow heartbeat.
A draft carries the scent of growing things -- impossible this deep. Fresh sap. Green leaves. It fades.
The stone beneath your feet vibrates with a rhythm that matches your heartbeat. When you stop, it stops.
A Blight-Hawk circles overhead in a vertical shaft, riding thermals. It watches you pass, but does not descend.

objective: Clear a dungeon floor of Hollow activity
Rescue a lost Seeker team from the lower tunnels
Retrieve a forge component from the Abandoned Forge
Scout a new Rot incursion in the Blightwood Hollow
Find a Blight cure sample from the Fungal Hermit
Deliver supplies to a forward outpost beyond the Charred Plains
Slay a specific creature that has been raiding the Emberhome Approaches
Investigate strange sounds from the Collapsed Cathedral
Recover an Arborist relic from a sealed Amber Vault
Map an unexplored passage in the Ironvein Mines
Escort a pilgrim safely to the Chapel of the Eternal Flame
Retrieve amber specimens for Elder Maven's research
complication: The DoomClock spikes unexpectedly during the mission
A second enemy faction arrives from an unexpected direction
The target has already been corrupted by the Blight
An NPC ally's secret is revealed at the worst possible moment
The environment shifts mid-mission -- the Burnwillow thrashes
Reinforcements cut off the escape route
A civilian is discovered in the crossfire
The real threat is hidden behind the obvious one
The objective is trapped or warded with Arborist defenses
A Rot Hunter begins tracking the party
reward: Tier-appropriate gear from the Warden's armory
Forge materials for Weld's upgrade bench
DoomClock reduction (-1d4) from Father Oaken's blessing
A new ally or recruit joins the party
Access to a previously sealed area
A rare crafting recipe decoded from a Memory Seed fragment
Permanent merchant discount from Birsa or Grimjaw
A lore revelation about the Burnwillow's history
An amber shard containing a memory of the Golden Age
A map fragment showing a shortcut through the Veins

5: The torches flicker. Distant growls echo through the stone.
Rot creatures in adjacent rooms become alert and begin patrolling.
A faint ashfall begins drifting from the ceiling.
The Root-Song carries a note of warning -- urgent, low, insistent.
10: Something stirs at the dungeon entrance. The hunt begins.
The Rot Hunter spawns at the entry room and begins tracking the party.
Corrupted NPCs in the zone become hostile.
The air thickens with spores. Visibility drops.
13: Patrol routes shift. Every enemy in the zone is now actively searching.
All enemy DC checks increase by +2.
New hazards appear in previously safe rooms.
A secondary entrance to the zone seals shut.
17: The Blight spreads visibly -- fungal growth covers walls in minutes.
HP cannot be restored naturally. Healing items work at half effect.
Tier escalation: all enemies gain +2 to hit and +2 to damage.
22: The dungeon seals. No escape. Root-Road exits lock.
Must fight to the zone boss or die trying.
The DoomClock cannot be reduced below 17.
All remaining Hollows in the zone converge on the party's position.

ambient_sounds: The slow drip of water on stone
A distant, rhythmic tapping -- metal on rock
The creak and groan of the Burnwillow settling
Scuttling in the walls, just out of sight
A subsonic hum that you feel more than hear
The whisper of ash settling on a flat surface
A faint, melodic sound -- the ghost of Root-Song
The click-click-click of Clockwork Spider legs
Something large breathing in the dark beyond the light
scavenge_finds: A handful of corroded copper coins, worthless but historical
A cracked amber chip containing a fragment of a child's laughter
An Arborist tool -- function unknown, handle warm to the touch
A Seeker's map fragment, partially accurate, partially wishful thinking
A sealed glass vial containing clear liquid that glows faintly blue
A bone trinket carved in the shape of a leaf, worn smooth by handling
Iron nails, straight and usable -- valuable as crafting material
A journal page describing a room that matches no known location
A fungal growth that, when crushed, emits a sharp white light for ten seconds
An amber bead on a leather cord -- Birsa would pay well for this
